summary: The race completed one full cycle with only the Scalper taking action, opening two crypto positions while the other three bots stayed in cash because the equity market was closed and their thresholds/universes blocked trades. Despite different behavior, the app declared a full tie because ranking is based only on immediate marked-to-market equity, which remained unchanged right after entry.
problems_found: Ranking logic is not useful in first-cycle races: all bots are tied at $250 even though Scalper deployed capital and others did nothing.

Three bots are structurally weak or inactive in after-hours mode because active_scan_symbols only included BTC-USD and ETH-USD while Conservative explicitly cannot trade crypto and the other two use thresholds that prevented any action.
Symbol normalization is inconsistent: scan universe uses BTC-USD/ETH-USD but executed/open position symbols are BTC/USD and ETH/USD.
Several reasoning_summary and log messages are truncated, suggesting UI/export clipping or storage length limits.
Scalper claims momentum qualification but no explicit volume/trend confirmation data is stored, even though Momentum Rider references rising volume as part of its rules.

data_fetch_started and data_fetch_completed timestamps are identical for all bots, which suggests diagnostics are not capturing real fetch duration or fetch work is happening outside measured instrumentation.
race_health shows bots_completed = 1 while all 4 bots completed their first cycle and have first_cycle_terminal=true; status semantics appear inconsistent.
No per-symbol indicator payload is exported beyond price, daily percent, and volume text, so it is impossible to audit why thresholds passed or failed in a machine-readable way.
No exit logic, stop loss, take profit, or next-action plan is visible for the Scalper's open crypto positions, making the race hard to evaluate beyond initial entry.
Summary reports leader as a tie and total_pnl 0, which hides the only meaningful distinction in this run: one bot actually executed trades.
recommended_changes: Add a race ranking fallback for flat-PnL first cycles that scores bots by action quality and deployment when equity is tied, e.g. executed_valid_trade > no_action_with_valid_reason > inactive_due_to_untradeable_universe.
Normalize symbol formatting across scanners, orders, positions, logs, and exports so one canonical symbol is used consistently for crypto and equities.

Expand export schema to include structured indicator data per considered symbol: last price, day change %, volume, volume trend/relative volume, eligibility, rejection reason, and threshold comparisons.
Fix diagnostics timing so data fetch, strategy evaluation, and order execution durations are measured independently and accurately.
Prevent truncated reasoning/log fields in exports and UI cards, or provide full_text fields alongside preview text.
Rework after-hours race behavior so each bot has a viable tradable universe in the current session, or mark bots as session-ineligible instead of letting them silently no-op.
Add explicit strategy metadata for each bot in export output, including entry thresholds, allowed asset classes, max allocation, and exit rules.
Add post-entry management fields for open positions such as stop, target, holding period intent, and next evaluation trigger.
Update race summary to surface non-PnL differentiators such as bots that traded, capital deployed, and number of valid opportunities identified.

Review Momentum Rider threshold calibration because +0.8% was only slightly above market conditions while Scalper traded on +0.3%; either make this intentionally distinct in UI or adjust to avoid near-duplicate behavior with different inactivity rates.
tests_to_run: Run another market-closed 4-bot race and verify the winner is not shown as a meaningless tie when one bot trades and others do not.
Verify BTC-USD/ETH-USD remain identical across scan inputs, order tickets, open positions, logs, and export JSON.
Confirm full reasoning_summary and latest_logs text is preserved in export and visible in the UI without truncation bugs.
Check race_health counters so bots_completed, bots_completed_first_cycle, and cycle terminal states are internally consistent.
Inspect exported indicator payloads for each considered symbol and confirm thresholds/rejection reasons are machine-readable.
Validate diagnostics durations are non-zero and reflect real fetch, strategy, and execution timing.
Run both market-open and market-closed races to ensure session-ineligible bots are clearly labeled rather than treated as generic no-action bots.
Verify summary surfaces capital deployed, executed trades, and actionable ranking rationale in addition to equity/PnL.
next_prompt_for_replit: Implement the race ranking and summary fix so first-cycle results are meaningful even when marked-to-market equity is tied. Update the race comparison logic to keep total_equity_marked_to_market as the primary ranking basis, but add deterministic tie-breakers in this order: 1) bots with successful filled orders rank above bots with no action, 2) among trading bots rank by percent of budget deployed into valid positions, 3) among non-trading bots rank bots with a valid session-compatible setup scan above bots whose strategy universe was unavailable in the current session, 4) then fall back to bot id for stability. Add explicit fields to the export summary and each bot result such as did_trade, capital_deployed, deployment_pct, session_eligible, and comparison_reason. Update the UI summary text so it no longer says TIE — all bots at par when one bot actually traded; instead explain that equity is tied but the leader won on tie-breakers. Preserve existing equity and pnl calculations. Also add unit tests covering: one bot trades while others no-op, multiple bots trade with equal equity but different deployment, and a market-closed race where a stock-only bot is marked session-ineligible.

summary: All four bots completed four cycles, but only Momentum Rider and Scalper placed trades because the app restricted trading to crypto after hours. Scalper actually has the highest marked-to-market equity and PnL, yet the race summary incorrectly names Momentum Rider as leader, while two bots were effectively inactive due to strategy-market mismatch.
problems_found: Race summary leader is incorrect: summary.leader says Momentum Rider, but Scalper has the highest total_equity ($250.2484 vs $250.0045).
Ranking data is inconsistent: Scalper has rank #1, but the top-level summary still reports a different leader.
race_health.bots_no_action is 4 even though Momentum Rider traded once and Scalper traded twice; this metric appears to reflect only the latest cycle while the field name implies whole-race status.
Top-level bots_completed is 0 while race_incomplete is false and all bots completed first cycle; completion semantics are inconsistent/confusing.
first_cycle_terminal is true for every bot even though cycles_completed is 4, suggesting a stale or incorrectly named flag.

Scalper strategy attempted invalid buys multiple times despite already being at the 30% symbol cap, meaning pre-trade validation is happening after AI decision generation instead of before order intent creation.
Scalper AI rationale is logically wrong: it says it can add roughly $24.81 while also acknowledging current ETH market value is already about $75.19, which already exceeds the $75 cap.
Conservative bot is structurally handicapped after hours: its allowed universe includes equities not available in the current session, so it is guaranteed to do nothing in crypto-only races.

Dip Buyer is also nearly non-participatory in this market state because only two crypto symbols are available and both were green; the race design does not ensure each bot has realistic opportunity to act.
Symbol formatting is inconsistent between input/watchlist and positions/orders: BTC-USD/ETH-USD in scan data vs BTC/USD/ETH/USD in positions and execution details.
Volume data quality looks inconsistent for ETH across cycles (117772k vs 1319k), indicating possible unit/normalization bug or mixed data sources.
Multiple reasoning_summary and info log messages are truncated mid-sentence, which hurts debugging and UI readability.

data_fetch_started and data_fetch_completed timestamps are identical for all bots and shared across bot runs, suggesting diagnostics are not capturing per-bot fetch duration accurately or are reusing a race-level timestamp.
The race is declared ready for comparison even though two strategies had no eligible universe under session constraints, reducing fairness of the comparison.
recommended_changes: Fix race summary aggregation so leader, rank order, tie handling, and ranking_basis are all computed from the same marked-to-market equity snapshot used for per-bot ranks.
Split no-action metrics into clearly named fields such as bots_no_action_this_cycle and bots_never_traded, and update UI/export semantics accordingly.
Add pre-decision exposure/cap checks to strategy context so bots do not propose impossible orders; if no capacity remains, they should emit HOLD/NO_ACTION without attempting a blocked trade.
Add a session-aware eligibility layer that marks each bot's tradable universe before the race starts and warns when a strategy has zero eligible symbols in the current market session.

Rebalance or redesign after-hours races so every bot has at least one valid asset class/universe; alternatively disable clearly incompatible bots or switch to a crypto-specific conservative and dip strategy after hours.
Normalize symbol formatting across the entire app to one canonical form, ideally preserving provider symbol externally and canonical internal asset IDs for positions/orders.
Normalize and validate market data units, especially volume fields, and add safeguards to prevent mixed-unit display in logs and reasoning.
Stop truncating reasoning_summary and latest_logs in exports/UI, or include full_text plus preview_text fields.
Clarify lifecycle flags by replacing ambiguous fields like first_cycle_terminal and bots_completed with explicit race_state and cycle_state values.
Improve diagnostics timestamps so each bot records actual fetch start/end and strategy execution durations independently.
tests_to_run: Run a 4-bot race after equity market close and verify the top-level leader matches the bot with highest total_equity.
Verify per-bot rank values, top summary leader, and tie labels all agree in cases with clear winner and near-tie.
Confirm bots_no_action_this_cycle and bots_never_traded are reported separately and correctly for bots that traded in earlier cycles but not the latest one.
Replay the Scalper scenario where current position market value slightly exceeds cap due to price movement and ensure the bot does not attempt any new buy.
Verify blocked order errors disappear for Scalper when no remaining symbol capacity exists and the bot instead logs a compliant HOLD reason.
Check that after-hours eligibility warnings appear for Conservative and any other strategy with zero eligible symbols.
Verify symbols are displayed consistently as one format across watchlist, logs, positions, execution details, and errors.
Inspect ETH/BTC volume values across consecutive cycles and confirm units remain consistent.
Ensure reasoning_summary and latest_logs export full untruncated text.
Validate diagnostics timestamps now show realistic per-bot fetch and strategy durations rather than identical reused values.
next_prompt_for_replit: Fix the AI Brain race ranking/summary bug so the top-level summary always matches per-bot marked-to-market equity ranks. In the current export, Scalper has the highest total_equity and rank #1, but summary.leader incorrectly says Momentum Rider. Please trace how race summary, ranking, tie detection, and leader selection are computed, then refactor so they all use one shared source of truth based on total_equity_marked_to_market. Also clean up related inconsistent fields: replace or clarify bots_no_action so it no longer implies whole-race inactivity when a bot traded in earlier cycles, and review bots_completed / first_cycle_terminal naming if needed to avoid misleading export data. Update TypeScript types, summary builders, and any UI components that display leader/rank/tie info. Add tests covering: clear winner, exact tie, within-$0.01 tie, bots with earlier trades but latest-cycle no action, and a regression test for this exact scenario where Scalper should be leader.

summary: The race completed cleanly with all 4 bots scanning 3 cycles, but only Scalper and Momentum traded during cycle 1 and then stalled because the overnight session had only BTC and ETH available. The winner was effectively decided by tiny marked-to-market gains plus deployment-based tie-breakers, while Dip Buyer and Conservative were structurally unable to participate in the crypto-only session.
problems_found: Scalper attempted an invalid repeat ETH buy in cycle 2 even though the symbol exposure cap was already reached, which means pre-trade candidate filtering is not enforcing portfolio constraints before order generation.
The app marks race_ready_for_comparison true even though all bots are in current NO_ACTION state and two strategies had no viable universe in this session, so comparison quality is weak.

Dip Buyer and Conservative are weak in overnight/crypto-only sessions because their allowed universe or entry rules make trading impossible; they are effectively dead agents outside regular equity hours.
first_cycle_terminal is true for every bot despite cycles_completed being 3 and bots still LIVE, which suggests a naming/logic mismatch in cycle state semantics.
bots_no_action is 4 in race_health while two bots clearly traded earlier; this metric appears to reflect only current cycle no-action but is labeled ambiguously and can be misread.
Symbol formatting is inconsistent across export fields: configured symbols use BTC-USD/ETH-USD while positions/orders/errors use BTC/USD and ETH/USD.
Several AI/info log messages are truncated in latest_logs/live_feed, indicating UI/export clipping and loss of diagnostic detail.
data_fetch_started and data_fetch_completed are identical for all bots, implying either reused shared market snapshot timing or missing true fetch duration instrumentation.

Ranking is based on total_equity_marked_to_market, but leader text says 'Equity tied' while ranks are still assigned 1 and 2 with non-identical equity values; tie messaging/epsilon handling is confusing.
There is no per-bot explanation field summarizing why a bot is session-ineligible versus strategy-no-signal versus constraint-blocked, making weak-agent diagnosis harder.
Missing market breadth/data context: no volatility, timeframe source, candle timestamps, or indicator values beyond simple day % and volume, which limits post-race analysis.
No evidence of exit evaluation details beyond narrative text; stop-loss/take-profit thresholds are mentioned in AI reasoning but not exported as structured strategy parameters or trigger checks.
recommended_changes: Fix order planning so bots cannot propose or attempt orders that violate symbol exposure caps, max position size, or available universe; blocked orders should be eliminated before execution and reflected as constraint-filtered candidates instead of errors.

Add session-awareness to bot eligibility and race comparison: mark bots as 'strategy unavailable this session' separately from 'no signal', and optionally exclude or downweight structurally ineligible bots in overnight races.
Normalize symbol formatting across all layers to one canonical form (prefer provider format plus display format mapping) so positions, logs, errors, and configured symbols match consistently.
Rename or split ambiguous metrics such as bots_no_action into bots_current_cycle_no_action and bots_never_traded, and align race_health with summary fields.
Replace or clarify first_cycle_terminal semantics so it reflects whether a bot stopped after cycle 1 versus simply finished its first cycle successfully.
Preserve full log text in exports and UI instead of clipping reasoning strings, especially for latest_logs and live_feed entries.
Export structured strategy metadata per bot: allowed universe, entry thresholds, stop-loss, take-profit, max positions, max symbol exposure, and why each candidate passed/failed.

Add a stronger overnight symbol set or session-specific fallback universes so Dip Buyer and Conservative can still compete outside equity hours, or explicitly schedule those bots only during compatible sessions.
Improve comparison logic to flag low-information races where most bots had no actionable universe, and surface a warning that results are not representative.
Record actual market snapshot timestamp/source and fetch duration per cycle so data freshness can be verified.
tests_to_run: Run a crypto-only overnight 4-bot race and verify no bot emits blocked-order errors for positions already at max symbol exposure.
Confirm Scalper and Momentum hold existing capped positions without attempting duplicate buys when only BTC and ETH are tradable.
Verify Dip Buyer and Conservative are labeled distinctly as 'no signal' versus 'session universe unavailable' and that race comparison reflects that status.
Check exported JSON for consistent symbol formatting across symbols, open_positions, execution_details, logs, and errors.
Confirm race_health metrics distinguish current-cycle no-action from never-traded bots and match summary totals.
Verify full AI/info log messages are preserved in export without truncation.
Check first_cycle_terminal or its replacement field for semantic correctness over multi-cycle races.
Validate leader/tie messaging when equity differences are below epsilon versus truly different.
next_prompt_for_replit: Fix the AI Brain race order-planning bug where bots generate orders that are guaranteed to be rejected by portfolio constraints. Specifically, implement pre-trade constraint filtering before any order attempt is created. In the current overnight crypto-only race, Scalper tried to buy ETH again even though ETH exposure was already at the $75 max symbol cap, producing a blocked-order error. I want the bot to recognize this earlier and simply log a no-action/hold decision without creating an invalid order attempt. Please: 1) find the order planning and constraint enforcement flow used by AI Brain race bots; 2) move symbol exposure cap, max position size, max open positions, and cash checks into candidate evaluation before order creation; 3) return structured reasons for candidate rejection (e.g. symbol_exposure_cap_reached) so the strategy can explain why it held; 4) ensure no blocked-order error is emitted for a constraint that should have been known pre-trade; 5) update export/logging so scan_result can include candidate_rejections and latest_logs mention filtered constraints instead of order rejection; 6) add or update tests covering a bot already holding max-sized BTC/ETH positions in a crypto-only session, verifying it does not attempt another buy and no error is recorded. Preserve existing strategy behavior otherwise.

summary: This race mostly stalled because equities were closed, leaving only BTC and ETH tradable; Dip Buyer and Conservative stayed fully in cash while Momentum and Scalper opened crypto positions earlier and finished slightly negative. The race comparison is technically complete, but the ranking and diagnostics are misleading because inactive no-trade bots were placed above bots with actual marked-to-market losses and several logs/data fields are inconsistent or truncated.
problems_found: Ranking logic is weak/misleading: Dip Buyer ranked #1 and Conservative #2 despite both doing nothing, while Momentum and Scalper actually took risk and have lower equity; the tie-breaker appears to reward inactivity/session flags in a way that produces unhelpful race outcomes.
Conservative has session_eligible=false but is still included in final ranking, which makes race comparison semantics unclear.
race_health shows bots_no_action=4 even though Momentum and Scalper previously traded; current-cycle NO_ACTION is being conflated with whole-race no-action behavior.
bots_completed=0 while all bots have first_cycle_terminal=true and race_ready_for_comparison=true, suggesting completion-state bookkeeping is inconsistent.
Symbol format is inconsistent between feeds and positions/orders: symbols list and scans use BTC-USD/ETH-USD while positions, execution_details, and errors use BTC/USD and ETH/USD.
Several reasoning_summary and info log messages are truncated mid-sentence, indicating export/UI clipping or storage length limits.
diagnostics.data_fetch_started and data_fetch_completed are identical timestamps for all bots, suggesting either missing fetch duration data or a shared cached fetch that is not represented clearly.
Conservative strategy reasoning references AMZN and NVDA, but those symbols are not in the race symbol list; strategy universe and configured race universe are out of sync.
Repeated ORDER BLOCKED errors show Momentum and Scalper keep attempting adds that violate exposure caps; agent decision logic is not pre-validating sizing before order submission.

Scalper reasoning says current open position value exceeds 30% guideline per position, but each individual position is near the cap, not above it by much; wording and sizing diagnostics are confusing.
No market context beyond simple daily percent and volume is provided, making it hard to judge whether no-trade decisions were reasonable or whether exits should have triggered.

active=false and STOPPED statuses appear after cycle 55, but there is no explicit stop reason in the export explaining whether the race ended naturally, was manually stopped, or hit a terminal-first-cycle rule.
recommended_changes: Fix race ranking/comparison logic so final ordering prioritizes actual performance and participation: rank by total_equity first, but if bots are within epsilon treat no-trade idle bots separately or rank traded bots ahead only when equity is meaningfully comparable; also exclude session-ineligible bots from podium placement or clearly bucket them.
Split current-cycle metrics from whole-race metrics: keep current_cycle_no_action separate from never_traded/no_action_entire_race so race health and summary are not misleading.
Normalize symbols across the entire system to one canonical format, preferably BTC-USD/ETH-USD, and convert at the broker boundary only.
Add pre-trade constraint validation in agent logic so strategies do not attempt orders that will obviously violate per-symbol exposure caps.
Stop truncating scan_result.reasoning_summary and latest_logs messages in exports; either store full text or provide explicit truncated=true metadata.
Clarify diagnostics by recording whether market data was shared/cached and include fetch source, staleness, and latency per cycle.
Synchronize each bot's allowed universe with the race symbol list, or explicitly report when a strategy references symbols unavailable in the configured race.
Add explicit race stop_reason and bot stop_reason fields so STOPPED can be interpreted correctly.
Improve comparison output by adding categories such as 'inactive due to market/session', 'held positions', and 'attempted blocked orders' to make weak agents obvious.
Add richer market snapshots per scan, including multi-timeframe momentum/drawdown and entry-vs-current return, so strategy decisions can be audited better.
tests_to_run: Run a crypto-only race with equities closed and verify final ranking clearly separates idle stock-only bots from active crypto bots.
Verify session-ineligible bots are either excluded from podium ranking or visibly labeled in a separate bucket.
Confirm race_health distinguishes bots that took no action this cycle from bots that never traded during the race.
Open positions in BTC/ETH and ensure all UI, exports, errors, and execution details use the same canonical symbol format.
Trigger exposure-cap scenarios for Momentum and Scalper and confirm no broker order is attempted when pre-validation already knows it will fail.
Export a race with long AI reasoning and verify no log lines or reasoning_summary strings are silently cut off.
Verify diagnostics now show meaningful fetch latency/staleness and correctly indicate shared cached market data if used.
Check that Conservative no longer references symbols outside the configured race universe unless explicitly labeled as off-universe strategy preferences.
Stop a race manually and let another race end naturally; verify stop_reason is correct in both exports.
next_prompt_for_replit: Please fix the AI Brain race ranking and summary logic so the output is not misleading in mixed-participation races. Specifically: 1) update the ranking engine to use total_equity as the primary rank, but when bots are effectively tied within epsilon, do not automatically reward idle/no-trade bots over bots that actually participated unless the product intentionally wants that and the reason is explicit; 2) treat session-ineligible bots as a separate category or exclude them from podium ordering; 3) split whole-race stats from current-cycle stats so bots_no_action does not count bots that traded earlier but took no action this cycle; 4) fix export summary fields so bots_completed, bots_current_cycle_no_action, bots_ever_traded, and comparison_reason are internally consistent; 5) update the summary UI/components to clearly label bots as Active Participant, Idle Due To No Signal, or Session-Ineligible. Please modify the ranking/service layer and any affected UI/export files, then add tests using a scenario like this export where two bots are idle in cash, one is session-ineligible, and two hold small losing crypto positions.

summary: All 4 bots completed their first cycle without hard failures, but only Scalper and Momentum Rider traded because the equity market was closed and only BTC/ETH were eligible. The race comparison is effectively meaningless so far because every bot is still at exactly $250 equity and the leader was chosen only by tie-breakers based on deployment rather than performance.
problems_found: Race ranking is premature and misleading: all bots have total_equity 250 and total_pnl 0, yet the app declares a leader based on deployment/tie-breakers instead of actual marked-to-market performance.
Two agents are structurally weak in this session: Conservative is session-ineligible when equities are closed, and Dip Buyer had no valid setup because only rising crypto symbols were available.
Strategy-universe mismatch reduces race fairness: 2 of 4 bots could not realistically participate in an overnight crypto-only session.
Only one cycle has completed for each bot, but first_cycle_terminal is true for all bots, which suggests the race may stop before strategies can manage exits or generate real PnL.
No post-trade price updates occurred after fills; unrealized_pnl stayed 0 because last_price equals fill_price, so there is no real marked-to-market comparison yet.
data_fetch_started and data_fetch_completed are identical timestamps for every bot, which looks like missing or placeholder diagnostics rather than real fetch timing.
scan_result.reasoning_summary strings are truncated for multiple bots, indicating export/UI clipping or serialization limits on rationale text.
latest_logs messages are also truncated in several places, causing loss of important context for debugging and review.
Symbol normalization is inconsistent: configured symbols use BTC-USD/ETH-USD while positions and fills use BTC/USD/ETH/USD.
Conservative strategy references symbols not present in the race universe (AMZN, NVDA), indicating a mismatch between configured symbols and strategy prompt/universe handling.
Race health says bots_completed is 2 while all 4 bots completed_first_cycle and none are in progress; completion semantics are unclear or buggy.
Tie-break labeling is confusing: is_tied is false on each bot, but tie_break_reason says equity within $0.01 and summary leader says 'Equity tied'.

Session eligibility logic is unevenly surfaced: Conservative has session_eligible false, Dip Buyer has true despite having no valid setup in the current market regime, which may distort comparison logic.
No errors were recorded, but multiple data-quality issues exist, so observability is too weak to distinguish true success from low-information runs.
recommended_changes: Change race comparison logic so bots are not ranked as winners until there is real performance separation: require either non-zero marked-to-market PnL, at least one post-entry repricing update, or completion of a minimum evaluation window/cycle count before declaring a leader.

Let races adapt to session context before launch: when equities are closed, either switch to crypto-capable bots only, substitute alternative bots, or mark the race as 'partial/unfair session' instead of directly comparing all 4.
Allow multi-cycle execution and exit management before first_cycle_terminal becomes true for momentum/scalping bots so they can realize strategy edge rather than only open positions.
Add a price-refresh/mark-to-market pass immediately after order fills and on subsequent intervals so unrealized PnL and ranking reflect live market movement.
Normalize symbol formatting across config, quotes, orders, positions, and logs to one canonical format, with broker-specific translation only at execution boundaries.
Fix export/log serialization limits so reasoning_summary and latest_logs preserve full text without truncation in exported race data.
Clarify race status fields: unify bots_completed, cycle_status, first_cycle_terminal, race_ready_for_comparison, and tie labels so the state model is internally consistent.
Update bot/session eligibility rules so bots that cannot trade the available universe are explicitly excluded from winner ranking rather than placed last as if they underperformed.
Align strategy prompts/universes with configured race symbols so bots do not mention unavailable assets like AMZN/NVDA unless those symbols are actually supplied.
Expand diagnostics with real fetch duration, quote age, fill latency, and mark-to-market timestamp so debugging can identify stale-data races.
tests_to_run: Run a 4-bot race during equity market closed hours and verify no winner is declared when all bots have identical equity and zero PnL.
Verify the UI/export shows a status like 'awaiting evaluation window' or 'tied/no performance data yet' instead of ranking by deployment alone.
Open crypto trades, then force a quote refresh and confirm last_price, market_value, unrealized_pnl, and total_equity update after fills.
Confirm symbol display is consistent everywhere as either BTC-USD/ETH-USD or BTC/USD/ETH/USD, with no mixed formats in one export.
Check that full reasoning_summary and latest_logs are preserved in the export without truncation.
Verify bots that cannot trade the session universe are marked excluded/ineligible for ranking rather than simply ranked last.
Confirm bots_completed, cycle_status, first_cycle_terminal, race_ready_for_comparison, is_tied, and leader text are internally consistent across the export and UI.
Run a daytime equities-open race and verify Conservative can participate when its allowed symbols are actually in the race universe.
Run a multi-cycle race and confirm open-position bots continue to manage exits instead of stopping after the first entry cycle.
next_prompt_for_replit: Implement a race-ranking fairness fix for the AI Brain 4-bot race. Right now the app declares a leader even when every bot has identical total_equity and total_pnl=0 after only the first cycle, using deployment-based tie-breakers. I want ranking and leader selection to require meaningful performance data. Please update the ranking/comparison pipeline so that: 1) if all bots are tied on total_equity within epsilon and there has been no non-zero realized or unrealized PnL and no post-fill mark-to-market update, the race should not declare a winner yet; 2) in that case return a neutral state such as 'awaiting evaluation window' or 'no performance separation yet'; 3) keep tie-breakers only for cases where there is real performance data but bots remain tied; 4) bots that are session-ineligible because their strategy universe is unavailable should be excluded from winner determination and clearly labeled as excluded/ineligible, not treated as losing performers; 5) make the exported summary fields internally consistent, including leader text, is_tie, race_ready_for_comparison, comparison_reason, and tie_break_reason; 6) update the leaderboard UI to show this neutral state clearly. Also add tests covering: identical equity after first cycle with zero PnL, identical equity after mark-to-market with real price movement, and a session where some bots are ineligible due to market hours. Keep existing data structures where possible, but add explicit fields if needed to represent 'comparison deferred' and 'excluded from ranking'.
